title: Polish Potato Bread
description: A hearty bread recipe inspired by Polish cuisine, featuring a potato twist.
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine yeast, warm water, and 1 tsp sugar. Let it sit for 5-7 minutes, until frothy.
2. Add flour, melted butter, egg, and salt to the bowl. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
3. Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10-12 minutes, until smooth.
4. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, until doubled in size.
6. Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
7. Place the loaf on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and bake for 35-40 minutes, until golden brown.
8. Let the bread cool on a wire rack for 10-15 minutes before slicing.
9. Serve warm, or let it cool completely for later use.
10. Store bread in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
